project: VERA: Support-Aware Shift Envelopes for Representation Erasure
scope: Secondary analyses of the already registered receipts. These analyses do not change the primary datasets, methods, seeds, candidates, attackers, splits, thresholds, deployment budget, decision rules, endpoints, or pass conditions.
outcome_access_at_lock: No aggregate real-study outcome report or candidate table had been generated. Individual receipts necessarily contain run-level external arrays, as disclosed in the analysis correction ledger.
selection_utility: Among eligible candidates, minimize maximum validation leakage, then maximum paired target harm, then candidate key, exactly as in the parent preregistration.

outputs: measured external contract-violation rate over all registered configurations
measured external contract-violation rate conditional on deployment
safe deployment retention relative to the measured external oracle
deployment_budget.rule: A candidate is eligible when its locked simultaneous radius is at least Gamma and all required deployment environments are supported.

outputs: point-selection and VERA measured external contract-violation rates

attacker_portfolio.rule: Recompute the simultaneous certificate from the same nested certification subsets. For each portfolio, allocate delta over exactly that portfolio's complete candidate-contract family. External safety is still evaluated against the full four-attacker contract, preventing a weaker audit from redefining success.
outputs: candidate-selection changes relative to the full portfolio

frontier_coverage.rule: Remove the named method from candidate availability while retaining the parent study's full-family confidence allocation. This isolates frontier availability from multiplicity changes.

outputs: unsupported environment count and zero-radius verification

inference.intervals: Seed-cluster bootstrap intervals are descriptive because only five seeds are available in the parent study.
inference.multiple_comparisons: No ablation p-value is promoted to a confirmatory claim. Primary inference remains governed by the parent preregistration and correction ledger.
inference.configuration_dependence: Thresholds and nested validation fractions share fits and examples; configuration rows are never treated as independent replicates.

pass_conditions.source_and_hash: This JSON and its SHA-256 sidecar must match.
pass_conditions.receipt_gate: The 125-run official receipt audit must pass before any ablation is generated.
pass_conditions.coverage: Every registered ablation cell is present or explicitly marked not estimable with a reason.
pass_conditions.provenance: Every output records the parent preregistration hash, this ablation hash, and source-table hashes.
pass_conditions.honesty: The paper labels all ablations secondary and does not use them to replace a failed primary endpoint.
